text: fat is 19.9g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. which is exactly what you want from a legume.
carbs is 30.2g. the fibre support is doing a lot of the work. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
protein is 36.5g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
vitamin c at 10% DV, vitamin e at 6% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 70% DV, iron at 49% DV, zinc at 37% DV stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

pros first. extreme protein/mineral legume because tests whether legumes can handle a very non-typical high-fat, high-protein member. very low glycemic behaviour because strong category fit. many practical uses because broad food-system relevance.

the drawbacks next. anti-nutrients matter because preparation and form matter a lot. often consumed in processed forms because whole soybeans are not the only real-world form. can be rough on some people because practical downside.
S tier. Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
